privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data generated by inject_references.py, which creates 10 legal references in a Jurism database at setup time. Two of the three target items are verified real Supreme Court cases: Obergefell v. Hodges (576 U.S. 644, 2015) and Gideon v. Wainwright (372 U.S. 335, 1963). The third target item, 'The Due Process Clause and the Substantive Law of Torts' by Ronald D. Poe (allegedly Yale Law Journal vol. 80, iss. 5, pp. 920-980, 1971), could not be verified despite exhaustive web searches and appears to be synthetic. The expected counts are correct: 3 items should be moved to trash and 7 should remain in the active library.
